# Critical Ranger FFM Pack — Zone-Control RL MVP

## Agent Start Here

Use this pack before Critical Ranger work. Active roadmap: zone-control RL MVP. Parked path: switch-point/single-cell efficacy, preserved only as diagnostic/prototype infrastructure. Next executable slice is the zone-control action contract fixtures issue.

## What This Pack Routes

- Current zone-control RL MVP roadmap.
- Parked/superseded switch-point efficacy work.
- Active repo, PRD, parent tracker, and next issue.
- Evidence gates, non-claims, and anti-cheat constraints.
- VPS vs local WSL execution guardrails.

## What This Pack Does Not Route

- Real wildfire validity.
- Policy or operational wildfire recommendations.
- GPU/Puffer/training/render commands on VPS.
- Local WSL gates without Jamie approving exact commands.
- Old switch-point path as final science proof.

## Source-of-Truth Hierarchy

1. **GitHub issues/PRs** — coordination truth.
2. **Zone-control PRD** — requirements truth.
3. **Repo code/tests** — implementation truth.
4. **Obsidian project Index** — durable synthesis.
5. **This pack** — routing truth.

## Canonical Sources

| Label | Path/URL | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Project hub | `Projects/Critical Ranger FFM/Index.md` | synthesis |
| Repo | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/critical-ranger-ffm` | implementation |
| Zone-control PRD | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/critical-ranger-ffm/blob/main/docs/PRD-zone-control-rl-mvp.md` | requirement |
| Parent tracker | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/critical-ranger-ffm/issues/53` | coordination |
| Next issue | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/critical-ranger-ffm/issues/54` | implementation |

## Current Narrative / Operating Posture

The main roadmap is zone-control RL MVP: test whether an RL agent can reduce mega-fires in a toy forest-fire model better than honest baselines without clearing the forest or over-spending interventions. The prior single-cell switch-point efficacy work is parked as diagnostic/prototype infrastructure.

Next gate: issue #54 action contract fixtures — deterministic zone indexing, no-op, one-zone thinning, and decision interval semantics. Do not jump to training or rendering.

## Do Not Do

- Do not run GPU/Puffer/training/render/raylib/c_render work on the VPS.
- Do not run local WSL HITL commands without Jamie approving the exact command.
- Do not claim final efficacy, SOC-control, publication-grade science, policy value, or real-wildfire validity.
- Do not delete parked switch-point artifacts.
- Do not implement from parent tracker alone; follow child issue scope.
- Do not overwrite README.md or docs/PRD.md without explicit instruction.

## Fallback Rules

1. If stale (>14 days), re-verify issue #53, issue #54, current PRD, and repo state.
2. If question touches old efficacy path, route to parked diagnostics and label it parked.
3. If question asks for real-world wildfire claims, refuse as out of scope.
4. If no match, fall back to Project Board then Projects MOC.

## Human Surface

- `Projects/Critical Ranger FFM/Index.md`
- `Project Board.md`
- `Maps of Content/Projects MOC.md`

## Maintenance Notes

Update when issues #54-#59 close, when a PRD changes, when main roadmap pivots, or when Jamie approves a HITL local run. Confidence is medium until post-#54 implementation facts are refreshed.
